team_context.competitive_window: transitioning — new HC Kevin Stefanski, new GM Ian Cunningham, new president Matt Ryan. 8th straight losing season (8-9). Trying to compete but clearly in a retool year with new regime evaluating talent.
team_context.cap_situation: tight — ~$16-20M in space after restructures of Matthews, Lindstrom, Terrell. $22.5M dead cap from Cousins release. No 2026 1st-round pick (traded to Rams for James Pearce Jr.). Only 5 draft picks.
team_context.trade_posture: New regime open to dealing non-core players for picks. GM Cunningham said 2026 will be 'the last year we ever have five picks.' Kyle Pitts on franchise tag is a potential trade chip. Young core (Robinson, London, Walker, Watts) is untouchable. Willing sellers of veteran contracts to recoup draft capital.

players.name: Michael Penix Jr.
players.performance_evidence: 60.1% completion, 1,982 yards, 9 TD/3 INT in 9 starts in 2025 before torn ACL. Flashed big arm talent but inconsistent, especially under pressure. 4-8 career record as starter across 2 seasons. Career 59.6% completion, 2,757 yards, 12 TD, 6 INT.

trade_value.reasoning: Former top-8 pick on a cheap rookie deal with 2 years of control plus 5th-year option. QB premium pushes value up, but ACL tear (3rd of career), inconsistent play, and uncertain status for Week 1 significantly cap it. Worth a late 2nd to a QB-needy team willing to bet on upside.

players.availability_reasoning: New regime (Cunningham/Stefanski/Ryan) did NOT draft him and signed Tua Tagovailoa as insurance/competition. However, both Ryan and Stefanski have publicly praised Penix. Dead cap of $13.5M makes a trade costly. More likely to compete for the job than be traded, but if they fall in love with a QB prospect or Tua excels, Penix could become available.
players.injury_notes: Partially torn left ACL in Week 11 of 2025. Surgery Nov 25, 2025. This is his 3rd ACL tear (right knee twice at Indiana in 2018 and 2020). Aiming for Week 1 return but timeline is tight (9-12 months). Significant long-term durability concerns with two surgically repaired knees.
players.trade_buzz: NFL.com's Jeffri Chadiha listed Penix as a 'loser' in free agency after Tua signing, noting new regime has 'no reason to tie themselves to a player who's been inconsistent and is now returning from a major injury.'

players.name: Kirk Cousins
players.performance_evidence: 61.7% completion, 1,721 yards, 10 TD/5 INT in 2025. Led team to 5-3 record as starter but QBR of 47.6 (tied 22nd). Benched in 2024 for poor play before taking back starting role when Penix got hurt.

trade_value.reasoning: Being released with post-June 1 designation. No trade value — Falcons eating $22.5M dead cap in 2026 and $12.5M in 2027. Will be a free agent.

players.availability_reasoning: GM Cunningham publicly stated at NFL Combine: 'We won't re-sign Kirk.' Being released on first day of league year. Effectively no longer a Falcon.
players.injury_notes: Recovered from 2023 Achilles tear. Held up physically in 2025.
players.trade_buzz: Will be released. NFL.com reports Cousins plans to play in 2026 elsewhere.

players.name: Bijan Robinson
players.performance_evidence: 5,605 career scrimmage yards through age 23 — most in NFL history before turning 24. Set Falcons franchise record for longest run (93 yards) and single-season scrimmage yards (surpassing William Andrews' 1983 mark). 2024 Pro Bowler. Elite runner, receiver, and pass protector. Has never missed a game.

trade_value.reasoning: Best RB in football on a dirt-cheap rookie deal ($7M cap hit) with 5th-year option available at just $11.4M. Despite RB positional devaluation, Robinson is a franchise centerpiece and offensive engine. Extension-eligible and Falcons will certainly pick up 5th-year option. Worth a top-10 pick equivalent but functionally untradeable.

players.availability_reasoning: CBS Sports' Jonathan Jones reported Falcons 'would never trade' Robinson. Called 'the best player in football' by former HC Morris. Franchise cornerstone — extension talks expected soon. New GM Cunningham's easiest decision will be picking up his 5th-year option.

players.name: Nathan Carter
players.performance_evidence: 5th-round rookie in 2025. Served as change-of-pace back behind Robinson and Tyler Allgeier. Limited production but showed flashes on special teams and in spot duty. Allgeier's departure to Arizona creates opportunity for expanded role.

trade_value.reasoning: Late-round rookie depth RB with minimal proven production. Essentially no trade value — worth a conditional 7th at most.

players.availability_reasoning: Depth piece. Team would part with him for any reasonable offer but unlikely to generate interest given lack of production.

players.name: Drake London
players.performance_evidence: 100 catches, 1,279 yards, 9 TD in 2024. 68 catches, 919 yards, 7 TD in just 12 games in 2025 (missed time with injury). Has only missed 1 game due to injury in his career otherwise. One of the NFL's best young WRs. Offense visibly collapsed when he was absent.

trade_value.reasoning: 24-year-old Pro Bowl-caliber WR1 entering final year of his 5th-year option. Would command $30M+ AAV on an extension. On 1 year of control the pick-equivalent reflects the talent but the contract risk — acquiring team would need to extend him immediately. Worth a mid-to-late 1st to a contender.

players.availability_reasoning: Extension talks are underway but there's a gap — London wants $30M+/year, Falcons hoping for ~$25M. Falcoholic reports negotiations 'could be reaching an impasse.' If extension can't get done, tag-and-trade is possible but Falcons clearly want to keep him. Not being shopped but has a price if talks stall.
players.injury_notes: Missed ~5 games in 2025, WR room collapsed without him. Generally very durable — missed only 1 game before 2025.
players.trade_buzz: Blogging Dirty reported negotiations with London 'could be reaching an impasse' with significant gap between player and team on AAV.

players.name: Jahan Dotson
players.performance_evidence: Career: 121 catches, 1,519 yards, 12 TD in 63 games across 4 seasons. Best year was rookie season (523 yards, 7 TD). Has underwhelmed since, with production declining after trade from Washington to Philadelphia. Former 1st-round pick who hasn't lived up to pedigree.

trade_value.reasoning: Former 1st-round pick on a modest 2-year deal, but has been a disappointment. Below-average production caps value. Worth a 4th-round pick at most to a team betting on a change-of-scenery bounce.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed in free agency so unlikely to be immediately traded, but was brought in as WR2/WR3 depth. If he doesn't produce, could become expendable quickly. New regime has no strong attachment.

players.name: Olamide Zaccheaus
players.performance_evidence: Career-high 533 yards in 2022 with Falcons. Had 39 catches, 313 yards, 2 TD with Bears in 2025. Returning to Atlanta as a depth/WR3/4 option. Familiar with the franchise from 2019-2022 stint. Speed and shiftiness in slot.

trade_value.reasoning: Veteran depth WR on a cheap deal. Minimal trade value — worth a conditional 7th at most.

players.availability_reasoning: Signed as depth. Would be easy to move if a better option emerges via draft or trade.

players.name: Kyle Pitts Sr.
players.performance_evidence: Bounced back in 2025 with 88 catches, 928 yards, 5 TD (career-high). Named 2nd-team All-Pro. ESPN called him the best TE statistically outside of Trey McBride. 1,026-yard rookie season in 2021 but middling years in between (MCL tear in 2022, sub-700 yards in 2023-24). Still only 25 with elite physical tools (6-6, 250).

trade_value.reasoning: Elite athletic talent at TE, just 25, coming off a 2nd-team All-Pro season. Franchise tag ($15M) is reasonable for the acquiring team. Worth a 2nd-round pick, but the 1-year control limits value. A team extending him immediately gets a potential top-3 TE through his prime.

players.availability_reasoning: On non-exclusive franchise tag — other teams CAN negotiate with him. CBS's Jonathan Jones reported teams 'could inquire' on Pitts. New GM Cunningham needs draft picks (only 5 in 2026) and Pitts' tag salary is a trade-friendly number. Long-term deal is preferred outcome but Falcons clearly hedging. Stefanski's TE-centric offense argues for keeping him, but tag gives leverage either way.
players.injury_notes: MCL tear in 2022 cost him most of that season. Healthy in 2024-2025.
players.trade_buzz: Jonathan Jones (CBS) reported teams could inquire on Pitts. GM Cunningham said team isn't 'in the business of letting go of really good players' but the tag is a prove-it year. Unclear if long-term deal will materialize by July 15 deadline.

players.name: Austin Hooper
players.performance_evidence: Veteran TE returning to Atlanta. Made Pro Bowl with Falcons in 2019 (75 catches, 787 yards). Has bounced around (Browns, Titans, Patriots, Raiders) since leaving. Now a veteran TE2/blocker.

trade_value.reasoning: 31-year-old TE2 on a cheap 1-year deal. Minimal trade value — depth piece.

players.availability_reasoning: Signed as depth/TE2 behind Pitts. Veteran leadership role. No significant trade demand expected.

players.name: Jake Matthews
players.performance_evidence: Starting LT since 2014 — remarkable longevity and durability. Ironman at the position. Dealt with some lower-body injuries in 2025 but continued to start. Solid, not elite, pass protector. Still a reliable starter but age (34 in 2026) is a concern.

trade_value.reasoning: Dead cap of $20M exceeds his cap hit, making him essentially untradeable. A 34-year-old OT with a bloated contract after restructure. Would cost MORE to trade him than to keep him.

players.availability_reasoning: Dead cap of $20M makes a trade financially impossible. Recent restructure added void year and locked Falcons in through 2027. Will start and retire as a Falcon.
players.injury_notes: Lower-body injuries in 2025. Generally very durable over 11 NFL seasons.

players.name: Matthew Bergeron
players.performance_evidence: Has started at LG since being drafted in 2023. Extension candidate per Spotrac. Solid all-around guard who has been a stabilizing force. Dealt with lower-body injuries in 2025 but started most games.

trade_value.reasoning: 25-year-old quality starting guard on a very cheap deal ($4.8M cap hit) with 2 years remaining. Extension candidate. Guards have lower positional value but the contract control and age make him attractive. Worth a late 2nd/early 3rd.

players.availability_reasoning: Young, cheap, proven starter — exactly the kind of player new regimes want to keep. Listed as extension candidate by Spotrac. Would only move for a clear overpay.

players.name: Ryan Neuzil
players.performance_evidence: Took over center job in 2025 after Drew Dalman's departure. Started 8 games in 2024 as a fill-in. UDFA who has worked his way into a starting role. Adequate but unspectacular.

trade_value.reasoning: Below-average starting center on a minimum deal. Worth a 6th-round pick at most. Replaceable.

players.availability_reasoning: Placeholder starter. If Falcons draft or sign an upgrade at center, Neuzil becomes a backup with minimal attachment.

players.name: Chris Lindstrom
players.performance_evidence: 2x Pro Bowl, 2x 2nd-team All-Pro. Highest-paid guard in NFL when extended ($105M/5yr in 2023). Elite run blocker per PFF. Only 1 holding penalty in first 4 NFL seasons. Consistent anchor of the OL. Dealt with lower-body injury in 2025 but remained one of the NFL's best guards.

trade_value.reasoning: Pro Bowl guard on 3 years of control, but massive dead cap ($30M) makes him functionally untradeable. His $26.3M cap hit is also significant. If he could be moved, he'd be worth a mid-1st, but the financials prevent it.

players.availability_reasoning: Dead cap ($30M) exceeds cap hit — trading him would cost MORE cap space than keeping him. Elite guard locked in through 2028. Recently restructured to create cap space, further cementing him in Atlanta.
players.injury_notes: Lower-body injuries in 2025. Missed games due to broken foot as a rookie (2019).

players.name: Kaleb McGary
players.performance_evidence: Consistently highly graded run-blocking tackle per PFF. Starting RT since 2019. Signed 2-year, $30M extension in August 2025 locking him through 2027. Lost to leg injury on final day of 2025 training camp — missed entire 2025 season.

trade_value.reasoning: Quality starting RT on a fair deal with 2 years remaining, but coming off a season-ending injury and turns 30. Dead cap of $10M makes a trade moderately costly. Worth a mid-3rd to a team needing OT help.

players.availability_reasoning: Just extended in August 2025 — Falcons are committed. But if he struggles returning from injury or the team drafts a tackle, could become available down the line.
players.injury_notes: Lost entire 2025 season to leg injury suffered in training camp. Returning for 2026.

players.name: Brandon Dorlus
players.performance_evidence: Versatile defensive lineman who plays 3-4 DE. Part of the young D-line core alongside Orhorhoro and Harrison, all age 24-25. Contributed as a run defender and occasional interior pass rusher. Starting role in 2025.

trade_value.reasoning: Young, cheap depth starter on a 4th-round rookie deal. Limited pass rush production caps value. Worth a 4th-5th round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Replacement-level starter on a cheap deal. New coaching staff will evaluate fit in their scheme. Could be moved for modest return.

players.name: Ruke Orhorhoro
players.performance_evidence: Clemson product selected 35th overall. Dealt with ankle injury in 2024 (placed on IR Week 8) but showed flashes when healthy. Key interior DL piece moving forward after David Onyemata's departure to Jets. Interior pass rush ability and athleticism.

trade_value.reasoning: 24-year-old 2nd-round pick DT on a cheap rookie deal with 3 years remaining. Upside is significant but hasn't fully broken out yet. Worth a 3rd-round pick given age, control, and potential.

players.availability_reasoning: Part of the young DL core the Falcons want to build around. Only 24 with 3 years of control on a cheap deal. Would take a strong offer to pry loose.
players.injury_notes: Ankle injury in 2024 — placed on IR. Returned later in season. Healthy for 2025.

players.name: Zach Harrison
players.performance_evidence: Role increased in 2025 — best statistical season but played only 7 games due to injuries. 30 tackles, 7 TFL, 5 sacks, 1 FF. Versatile 3-4 DE. Only 24 and part of young DL core. In contract year.

trade_value.reasoning: Young, cheap, flashing upside as a pass rusher but limited by injuries. Final year of contract limits control. Worth a 4th-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Contract year player. If Falcons can get fair value in a trade and replace via draft/FA, they might do it rather than risk losing him for nothing in 2027.
players.injury_notes: Played only 7 games in 2025 due to injuries (limited to 240 defensive snaps).

players.name: Jalon Walker
players.performance_evidence: Rookie in 2025: 36 tackles, 5.5 sacks, 2 forced fumbles, 29 pressures. Versatile — plays off-ball LB and on the edge. Missed only 5% of tackle attempts. 'Quietly very good' per Falcoholic. Butkus Award winner at Georgia. Enormous ceiling at just 22.

trade_value.reasoning: 22-year-old 1st-round EDGE/LB on 4 years of cheap rookie control plus 5th-year option. Already productive as a rookie with elite traits and scheme versatility. EDGE premium + youth + contract control = enormous value. Worth a mid-to-high 1st-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Franchise building block. Only 22 with 4 years of control. Coaches and analysts alike project him as a future All-Pro. With Pearce's legal troubles, Walker is even MORE critical to the defense. Zero chance of being traded.

players.name: Divine Deablo
players.performance_evidence: 73 tackles, 1 sack, 7 PD, 5 TFL in 13 games in 2025. Team went 8-4 with him and 0-5 without him — enormous impact on defensive performance. Opponents scored 6 more PPG without Deablo. Versatile, athletic middle linebacker.

trade_value.reasoning: Quality starting LB in contract year at age 27. Impact is clear — team's record with/without him tells the story. $8.9M cap hit is reasonable. Worth a 3rd-round pick but contract year limits value.

players.availability_reasoning: Contract year player. Team clearly values him (went 0-5 without him). If extension can't happen, he could be trade bait at deadline. For now, key piece of the defense under new DC.
players.injury_notes: Missed 4 games in 2025 including a broken forearm. Has not played a full 17-game season since 2021.

players.name: JD Bertrand
players.performance_evidence: UDFA who has worked into a rotational/starting ILB role. Run-stuffing linebacker with limited coverage ability. Notre Dame product. Contributed on special teams.

trade_value.reasoning: Depth LB on a minimum deal. Essentially no trade value.

players.availability_reasoning: Replacement-level depth. Would move for any pick offered.

players.name: James Pearce Jr.
players.performance_evidence: Sensational rookie: 10.5 sacks (most by a Falcon rookie ever, most by any rookie since Micah Parsons), 26 tackles, 5 PD, 1 FF, 1 FR. Named NFL Defensive Rookie of the Month in December. Finalist for DROY. Led all rookies in QB pressure rate (14.7%). Pure speed rusher with elite first step.

trade_value.reasoning: Would normally be worth a high 1st given elite EDGE production on a cheap 4-year rookie deal at age 22. However, 5 felony charges (aggravated battery, stalking, fleeing police) crater his trade value massively. Possible NFL suspension under personal conduct policy. Some teams won't touch him. Worth a 2nd-round pick to a team willing to take the risk, but could become worthless if convicted/suspended long-term.

players.availability_reasoning: Five felony charges including two counts of aggravated battery with a deadly weapon and aggravated stalking. Falcons said they're 'gathering more information.' Falcoholic reports his future with the team is 'extremely cloudy.' NFL confirmed review under personal conduct policy. Commissioner's exempt list or multi-week suspension is likely. Possible release if convicted. Despite the on-field talent, the off-field situation makes him potentially available at a discount, though the Falcons traded away their 2026 1st-round pick to draft him, so there's strong reluctance to give up on the investment.
players.character_notes: Arrested Feb 7, 2026 on 5 felony charges: 2 counts aggravated battery with deadly weapon, aggravated stalking, fleeing/eluding police, aggravated battery of law enforcement officer. Also 9 traffic violations and misdemeanor resisting arrest. Allegedly rammed ex-girlfriend Rickea Jackson's car with his Lamborghini while she tried to flee to police station. At least 7 prior 911 calls documented in weeks before arrest. Pre-trial protective order issued. Jackson says she will testify. Attorney says Pearce 'maintains innocence.' NFL reviewing under personal conduct policy.
players.trade_buzz: The Falcoholic noted his future is 'extremely cloudy' and the team may determine they 'don't want Pearce.' However, team hasn't publicly indicated intent to release. Dead cap of ~$9M and sunk cost of a traded 1st-round pick complicates matters.

players.name: A.J. Terrell Jr.
players.performance_evidence: 2021 2nd-team All-Pro. 4-year, $81M extension ($65.8M guaranteed) signed Aug 2024 — 2nd-highest paid CB at signing. Shadow corner who follows top receivers. Consistent, durable presence (started 61 of 67 career games). Career: 247 tackles, 43 PBU, 4 INT.

trade_value.reasoning: 27-year-old Pro Bowl CB on 3 years of a $20.25M AAV deal. Elite corner on a market-rate contract. Would be worth a late 1st/early 2nd, but dead cap of $38M makes him completely untradeable.

players.availability_reasoning: Dead cap of $38M makes any trade financially impossible. Franchise cornerstone on defense. Recently restructured to create cap space, further burying guaranteed money into future years.

players.name: Mike Hughes
players.performance_evidence: Former 1st-round pick (Vikings, 2018) who has bounced around. Extended by Falcons after solid 2024. Fell off in 2025 — played only 12 games due to injuries and performance didn't match 2024. Below-average CB2.

trade_value.reasoning: Below-average CB2 on a modest deal. Former 1st-round pedigree means some team might take a flyer. Worth a 5th-6th round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: If Falcons draft a CB or sign a better option, Hughes becomes easily expendable. Not a core piece. New regime didn't sign him.
players.injury_notes: Neck injury in 2025, played only 12 games. Injury concerns throughout career.

players.name: Billy Bowman Jr.
players.performance_evidence: Rookie in 2025: Started as nickel corner immediately. Had 1.5 sacks as a DB. Part of impressive rookie class that accounted for turnovers. Oklahoma product with ball skills and versatility.

trade_value.reasoning: 23-year-old starting nickel corner on a cheap 4-year rookie deal. Showed promise as a rookie. Worth a 3rd-round pick given age and control.

players.availability_reasoning: Young, cheap, promising. Exactly the type of player a rebuilding/retooling team keeps. Would take a significant overpay.
players.injury_notes: Torn Achilles late in 2025 season. Recovery timeline for 2026 is uncertain.

players.name: Jessie Bates III
players.performance_evidence: 2x 2nd-team All-Pro in 3 seasons with Falcons. Career-high 6 INT in 2023. Incredibly durable (5+ straight 1,000-snap seasons). Signed 4-year, $64M deal in 2023. Elite range safety who can play single-high or near the LOS. Anchor of the secondary.

trade_value.reasoning: 2x All-Pro safety, but age 29 with a $24.8M cap hit in final year of contract and $12M dead cap if traded. The cap hit is enormous for a safety. A contender who could absorb the salary would pay a 2nd-round pick. But most teams would want a restructure.

players.availability_reasoning: Entering final year of his deal at age 29 with a $24.8M cap hit. SI.com asked 'Is a $25M safety a luxury this team can afford?' Restructure candidate (Falcons can still convert salary to bonus). If they can't restructure or extend, he becomes a trade candidate for cap relief. But he's the defense's best player.
players.trade_buzz: Listed as restructure candidate by multiple outlets. No active trade buzz but the massive cap hit invites speculation.

players.name: Xavier Watts
players.performance_evidence: Outstanding rookie season: 5 INT (tied 2nd in entire NFL), 1,000+ defensive snaps. PFF's best coverage-graded rookie safety (75.5). Made PFF's All-Rookie team. Notre Dame product. Led Falcons rookies in snaps. Allowed just 21 catches on 623 coverage snaps.

trade_value.reasoning: 24-year-old starting FS who tied for 2nd in the NFL in INTs as a rookie, on a cheap 4-year deal. Safety premium is low but his ball production and youth are exceptional. Worth a high 2nd-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Franchise building block. 24 years old, 4 years of cheap control, immediate starter, ballhawk. Perfect complement to Bates. Zero reason to trade him.

players.name: Nick Folk
players.performance_evidence: Led NFL in FG accuracy in 2025 (96.6%). 18-year veteran, 16th most points in NFL history. 85% career FG, 96.9% XP. Elite accuracy and consistency.

trade_value.reasoning: Kickers have no meaningful trade value. Skip.

players.availability_reasoning: N/A — kickers not traded.

players.name: Tua Tagovailoa
players.performance_evidence: Led NFL in completion percentage in 2023 (69.3%), posted a 96.2 passer rating that year with 4,624 yards and 29 TDs. However, 2024 season was severely disrupted by another concussion (third documented NFL concussion, placed on IR), limiting him to 10 games. When healthy has shown Pro Bowl-caliber accuracy and efficiency in Miami's scheme. Career 64.8% completion, 89.5 passer rating. Production tier is capped by durability concerns and the fact he is now a backup on ATL's depth chart behind Kirk Cousins/Michael Penix Jr.

trade_value.reasoning: Tua has starter-level talent and 3 years of control, but the massive contract ($53M AAV) and severe concussion history significantly suppress his value. A QB-needy team would view him as a potential franchise answer at a steep financial cost with major medical risk. Comparable to a late 1st/early 2nd in talent, but contract and health drop him to a mid-to-late 2nd equivalent.

players.availability_reasoning: Atlanta acquired Tua in the 2025 offseason but he sits behind Michael Penix Jr. as QB2. The Falcons committed to Penix as the future when they drafted him 8th overall in 2024 and eventually moved on from Kirk Cousins. Carrying Tua's $43M+ cap hit as a backup is untenable. Multiple reports indicate ATL would move Tua for reasonable draft capital. The issue is finding a trade partner willing to absorb the contract given the concussion history.
players.injury_notes: Extremely significant concussion history — at least 3 documented NFL concussions (2022 twice, 2024) plus college history. Placed on IR in 2024 for concussion. This is the single biggest factor suppressing his trade value. Any acquiring team would need to be comfortable with the long-term neurological risk and the possibility of another concussion ending his career.
players.trade_buzz: Widely reported as available since Atlanta's offseason moves. Multiple outlets (ESPN, NFL Network) have noted that teams have called on Tua but the concussion history and contract have made finding a match difficult. Teams like the Giants, Raiders, and Browns have been speculatively linked but no deal has materialized as of June 2025.

players.name: Da'Shawn Hand
players.performance_evidence: Hand has carved out a career as a run-stuffing interior lineman who provides solid gap discipline but limited pass rush. Originally a promising 4th-round pick with Detroit in 2018, injuries derailed his early career (ankle, elbow issues limited him to 17 games across 2019-2020). Has been a journeyman since, with stints on multiple rosters. Starting at DT1 in Atlanta reflects more about the team's depth chart needs than his individual dominance. Likely 25-35 tackles, 1-2 sacks projection. Adequate starter, not a difference-maker.

trade_value.reasoning: Expiring contract on a modest deal, below-average starter-level production at a low-value position. Worth a 6th-round pick at best. Most teams would rather sign a comparable player in free agency than trade for him.

players.availability_reasoning: Veteran on a one-year deal with minimal dead cap. Atlanta has no long-term investment in Hand and would move him for any reasonable draft compensation, particularly if they fall out of contention or if a younger player emerges on the roster.
players.injury_notes: Significant early-career injury history with Detroit — ankle injury (2019), elbow injury requiring surgery (2020). Has been healthier in recent seasons but the injury-prone label has followed him and suppresses trade interest.
